description: An association is read inside a loop without eager loading, issuing one query per row and overloading the database.
body: Failure mode: a view or service iterates a collection and accesses record.association per item without .includes, so an N-row page fires N+1 queries, fine with seed data and crippling in production. Detection signals: the log shows the same query repeated per row; the bullet gem warns of N+1; list endpoints slow as data grows. Do not access associations per row without preloading. Safer fallback: add .includes(:assoc) (or eager_load when filtering on the association) at the feeding query, batch large sets with find_each, and add a query-count spec asserting a constant count regardless of rows.
